summary_one_line: Input needs a valid sfnt header with a non-zero table count and a recognized 4-byte table tag so the array sanitize loop has elements to iterate.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed OpenType header: magic 00 01 00 00 at offset 0, a non-zero numTables/count field (offsets 0x04-0x05), and one of the literal 4-byte sfnt/AAT table tags (e.g. 'feat', 'trak', 'name', 'VORG') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, so sanitize_shallow's check_array succeeds and count>0 makes the for-loop enter its body (true side).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the 00 01 00 00 sfnt magic, a non-zero count byte at offset 0x05, and ASCII table tags at 0x0c-0x0f ('feat','trak','name'), the exact bytes whose CMP against harfbuzz's known-tag constants the I2S stage can satisfy by substitution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 00 in offsets 0x05-0x10 (count and tag region all zero), so the tag never matches a recognized table and count stays 0, taking the false side and never entering the loop body.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace: cmplog buffers the failing tag/count CMP operands per execution and substitutes the harfbuzz-side comparison constant (the table tag, the count check) directly into the input, planting the literal bytes that plain value_profile's Hamming-distance feedback cannot reliably assemble at these multi-byte tag offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the loop-entry test of an array sanitize over font table records; the true side is globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:     for (unsigned int i = 0; i < count; i++)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Entering the loop body requires sanitize_shallow to succeed via len.sanitize and check_array; the body is only hit by the winner.
The 1-hop caller gates the loop on check_array(arrayZ, len), reached only by the winner.
Winner seeds carry the 00 01 00 00 sfnt magic and an ASCII table tag ('feat') at offsets 0x0c onward.
Another winner seed carries the 'trak' table tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
Loser-blocking seeds are all-zero across the count and tag region (offsets 0x05-0x10), so no recognized tag and count=0.
The byte diff shows winner carries printable tag bytes at 0x0c-0x0f while loser carries 00 there.
At offset 0x05 the winner carries a non-zero count byte (mostly 01) while the loser carries 00.
value_profile_cmplog's mutator set adds I2SRandReplace on top of havoc/token, which value_profile lacks.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5397 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed c62e53eef0209e96` and observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 16 of the 17-level ancestor chain, the characteristic unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If value_profile resolves the branch at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog when the harness front-matter (sfnt magic + non-zero count + a recognized 4-byte table tag) is supplied pre-formed in the seed, showing the divergence is not driven by I2S supplying the multi-byte tag/count operands.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff is multi-tag and noisy (different winner seeds use feat/trak/name/VORG), so I cannot pin the divergence to a single fixed tag literal versus the more general 'any recognized tag + non-zero count' structural requirement.
